source.title: Rapid response to fast viral evolution using AlphaFold 3-assisted topological deep learning

gist_memory.paper_problem_memory: Fast viral evolution (e.g., SARS-CoV-2) outpaces experimental methods for deep mutational scanning (DMS) and 3D structure determination, hindering timely forecasting of dominant variants. The paper addresses this by proposing AlphaFold 3 (AF3)-assisted multi-task topological Laplacian (MT-TopLap) to predict DMS and binding free energy (BFE) changes using AF3-predicted structures instead of experimental ones.

section_gists.heading: Abstract
section_gists.gist: Proposes AF3-assisted MT-TopLap to predict DMS and BFE changes for rapid viral response. Validation on four SARS-CoV-2 RBD-ACE2 DMS datasets shows robust performance with AF3 structures: average 1.1% decrease in PCC and 9.3% increase in RMSE vs. experimental structures. Achieves PCC 0.81 on HK.3 variant, demonstrating adaptability.
process_signals: Problem: fast viral evolution challenges experimental DMS and structure determination.
Hypothesis: AF3 structures can substitute experimental ones for TDL-based prediction.
Validation: cross-validation on four DMS datasets, comparison with experimental structures.
Performance: minimal drop in PCC, moderate increase in RMSE.
Generalization: tested on new HK.3 variant with PCC 0.81.
methods: AF3-assisted MT-TopLap: combines persistent Laplacians, auxiliary features, transformer features.
Multi-task learning with SKEMPI 2.0 and DMS data.
failures_or_limits: Average 9.3% increase in RMSE when using AF3 structures.

section_gists.heading: 1 Introduction
section_gists.gist: Motivates need for computational methods due to slow experimental DMS and structure determination. TDL has shown success in forecasting variants but requires experimental DMS and 3D structures. MT-TopLap integrates persistent Laplacian, auxiliary, and ESM-2 transformer features, improving over prior models. AF3 is proposed to supply structures for new variants, enabling rapid response.
process_signals: Problem: experimental DMS and structures are bottlenecks; e.g., first DMS took 9 months.
Prior work: TDL predicted DMS in silico earlier, but experimental data needed for high accuracy.
Limitations of persistent homology addressed by persistent Laplacian.
MT-TopLap design: multi-task learning with SKEMPI 2.0 and DMS, integrating PL, auxiliary, transformer features.
Hypothesis: AF3 can provide timely 3D structures for TDL.
methods: Multi-task deep learning with SKEMPI 2.0 and DMS
failures_or_limits: Persistent homology lacks non-topological shape evolution description.
TDL requires high-quality 3D structures, which are often delayed.

section_gists.heading: 2 Results
section_gists.gist: Evaluates AF3-assisted MT-TopLap on four SARS-CoV-2 RBD-ACE2 DMS datasets (6M0J-RBD-1, 6M0J-RBD-2, 7T9L-RBD, 7XB0-RBD). Compares performance using AF3 vs. experimental (PDB) structures. AF3 structures yield average 1.1% PCC decrease and 9.3% RMSE increase. Best PCC 0.968 on 7XB0-RBD with AF3. Also shows predicted DMS for HK.3 variant.
process_signals: Datasets: two original SARS-CoV-2 and two Omicron subvariants (BA.1, BA.2).
Performance comparison: AF3 vs. PDB structures.
Observation: minimal PCC drop, moderate RMSE increase.
Best case: 7XB0-RBD PCC 0.968 (0.6% lower than PDB).
HK.3 variant prediction shown (PCC 0.81 mentioned in abstract).
failures_or_limits: Average 9.3% RMSE increase indicates some loss of accuracy with AF3 structures.

section_gists.heading: 3 Discussion
section_gists.gist: Discussion section heading only; no content provided.

section_gists.heading: 4 Methods
section_gists.gist: Describes data: four experimental SARS-CoV-2 RBD-ACE2 DMS datasets and SKEMPI 2.0 for multi-task training. AF3 used to predict 3D complexes. Details in Supporting Information.
process_signals: Multi-task training: SKEMPI 2.0 (BFE changes) + DMS data.
AF3 structures generated via AlphaFold Server.

section_gists.heading: 5 Conclusion
section_gists.gist: Summarizes AF3-assisted MT-TopLap strategy to overcome lack of experimental DMS and structures. Validated on four DMS datasets, showing robust performance with AF3 structures. Extends to HK.3 variant with PCC 0.81. Highlights potential for rapid response to viral evolution.
process_signals: Problem: bottleneck of experimental DMS and 3D structures.
Solution: AF3-assisted MT-TopLap.
Validation: four DMS datasets, minimal deviation from experimental structures.
Generalization: HK.3 variant PCC 0.81.
Future: rapid response capability.
failures_or_limits: Need for experimental validation mentioned in abstract but not detailed here.

global_hypotheses_about_storyline: The research evolved from the need to accelerate viral variant forecasting by replacing slow experimental steps with computational predictions.
The core hypothesis is that AF3-predicted structures can effectively substitute experimental structures in TDL models for DMS and BFE prediction.
MT-TopLap was designed to integrate multiple feature types (topological, geometric, evolutionary) and multi-task learning to improve accuracy.
Validation on multiple datasets and a new variant demonstrates the approach's robustness and adaptability, supporting its use in rapid response scenarios.
uncertainties_to_resolve: How does performance vary across different structural regions (interior vs. surface) and mutation types? (Discussion subsections not provided)
What are the specific limitations of AF3 structures (e.g., accuracy in certain regions) and their impact on predictions?
What is the computational cost of generating AF3 structures and running MT-TopLap?
Are there any failure cases where AF3 structures lead to significantly worse predictions?
How does the model perform on non-SARS-CoV-2 viruses or other PPIs?
What revisions or iterative improvements were made to the model (e.g., feature engineering, architecture changes)?

events.summary: Experimental DMS and 3D structures are bottlenecks for rapid response to viral evolution.
AF3-predicted structures can substitute experimental structures for TDL-based DMS prediction.
MT-TopLap integrates persistent Laplacian, auxiliary, and pre-trained transformer features for multi-task learning.
Validation on four SARS-CoV-2 RBD-ACE2 DMS datasets using AF3 vs. experimental structures.
AF3 structures yield average 1.1% PCC decrease and 9.3% RMSE increase compared to experimental structures.
Fine-tuning validation on HK.3 variant DMS dataset achieves PCC 0.81 and RMSE 1.10.
TDL requires high-quality 3D structures, which are often delayed during pandemics.

Fine-tuned model predicts DMS for HK.3 variant with PCC 0.81 and RMSE 1.10, resembling experimental patterns.

Analysis of BFE changes by mutation types shows predicted patterns align with experimental data, but variance is lower.
Tracking SARS-CoV-2 variants using predicted BFE changes; Q493E mutation effect reversed in KP.3 variant.

chunk_events.notes: Chunk contains detailed validation, analysis, and revision events for AF3-assisted MT-TopLap, including performance metrics, structural region analysis, mutation type analysis, and variant tracking.

events.summary: Persistent Laplacian features are generated from AF3-predicted 3D SARS-CoV-2 RBD-ACE2 complex using element- and site-specific atom sets to encode topological invariants.
ESM-2 pre-trained transformer converts amino acid sequence of PPI into embedding, concatenated with other features for MT-TopLap.
MT-TopLap deep neural network has six hidden layers with 15,000 neurons each, with an output channel per dataset.
Dataset-level 10-fold cross-validation performed on four experimental SARS-CoV-2 RBD-ACE2 datasets with specific learning rates and epochs.
Persistent Laplacian features are generated using Vietoris-Rips complex for dimension 0 and Alpha complex for dimensions 1 and 2, with specific filtration parameters.
Statistical values of harmonic and non-harmonic spectra are computed to form persistent Laplacian feature vectors of specific sizes.
AF3-assisted MT-TopLap achieves robust predictive performance on four DMS datasets with minimal deviation from experimental structures, and extends to HK.3 variant with PCC 0.81.

chunk_events.notes: Chunk contains detailed design of AF3-assisted MT-TopLap, feature generation methods, and validation results, directly supporting the paper's core methodology and findings.

evidence_bank.quote: the DMS experiments are typically a bottleneck in response to fast evolving viruses
the earliest experimental DMS on the SARS-CoV-2 spike protein RBD mutations was not available until September 2020, nine months after the outbreak
We propose an AlphaFold 3 (AF3)-assisted multi-task topological Laplacian (MT-TopLap) strategy to address this need.

we leverage AF3's predicted structures to extract persistent Laplacian features. As such, we can speed up in-silico DMS predictions by bypassing the experimental determination of PPI complex structures.
MT-TopLap integrates persistent Laplacian, auxiliary, and pre-trained transformer features to predict BFE changes upon RBD DMS mutations.

SKEMPI 2.0 is used as training data with a multi-task deep learning approach that simultaneously learns single-site mutational patterns with experimental BFE changes and DMS profiles with experimental enrichment ratios.
We validate our AF3-assisted MT-TopLap approach with four experimental RBD-ACE2 DMS datasets of SARS-CoV-2 and its variants

we first collected four experimental RBD-ACE2 DMS datasets, each paired with an experimental 3D SARS-CoV-2 RBD-ACE2 complex structure. Meanwhile, we also create alternative AF3 3D RBD-ACE2 complex structures from the AlphaFold 3 Server.

AF3-assisted MT-TopLap maintains strong performance in predicting BFE changes upon DMS mutations, with an average decrease of only 1.1% in Pearson correlation coefficient (PCC) and an average increase of only 9.3% in RMSE, compared to the use of experimental RBD-ACE2 structures.
AF3-assisted MT-TopLap achieves a PCC of 0.81 and RMSE of 1.10 for this fine-tuning validation.
MT-TopLap requires the 3D structures of new viral variants to achieve accurate predictions. Such structures are typically not available until many months later during pandemics.
AF3-assisted MT-TopLap only displayed an average decrease of 1.1% in PCC when compared to using Protein Data Bank (PDB) structures.
the four SARS-CoV-2 RBD-ACE2 datasets displayed an average of 9.3% increase in RMSE when validated with AF3-assisted MT-TopLap.
AF3-assisted MT-TopLap also achieved the best performance in the validation test for 7XB0-RBD with a PCC of 0.968, only 0.6% lower than the performance using PDB complexes.
AF3-assisted MT-TopLap's predicted BFE changes for 7XB0-RBD obtained the lowest RMSE of 0.338 kcal/mol
To enhance AF3-assisted MT-TopLap's capabilities using experimental DMS data, we use transfer learning to update AF3-assisted MT-TopLap by training it with latest experimental DMS data.
we use the AlphaFold Server to predict the 3D structure of HK.3 variant RBD-ACE2 complex.
The predicted BFE changes are obtained by performing a 10-fold cross-validation after MT-TopLap has been pre-trained with the SARS-CoV-2 RBD-ACE2 datasets and the S8338 dataset.
The patterns of the predicted DMS is observed to resemble the patterns of the converted BFE changes with a PCC of 0.81 and an RMSE of 1.10.

The combined prediction results for different residue region types for four AF3-assisted SARS-CoV-2 RBD-ACE2 datasets according to Fig. 1(a) with PCCs of 0.947, 0.943, and 0.931 for the binding interfaces: support, core, and rim respectively.
the variance of the predicted BFE changes are generally lower than the variance of the experimental values
in the current KP.3 variant, when Q493E is combined with L455S and F456L mutations, the effect has reversed thus promoting an increase in BFE changes

the AF3 generated 3D SARS-CoV-2 RBD-ACE2 complex is used to generate persistent Laplacian features and auxiliary features. Persistent Laplacian features provide a topological representation, using element-and site-specific atom sets to simplify the structural complexity of protein–protein complexes and encode vital biological information into topological invariants.

The ESM-2 pre-trained transformer is used to convert amino acid sequence of the PPI into a pre-trained transformer embedding. All these features are concatenated and fed into MT-TopLap to predict the BFE change upon mutation.
The deep neural network model in MT-TopLap's architecture consists of six hidden layers with 15,000 neurons in each layer and generates an output channel for each dataset.

we perform dataset-level 10-fold cross-validation on four experimental SARS-CoV-2 RBD-ACE2 datasets. The learning rate is set to 0.0001 and 500 epochs are used for the pre-training while 200 epochs are used for the validation step.
For generating our persistent Laplacian-based features, we utilized the Vietoris-Rips complex for dimension 0 and the Alpha complex for dimensions 1 and 2.
Using filtration with the Rips complex and DI distance, we generate 0-dimensional PL features ranging from 0Å to 6Å with a grid size of 0.5Å.

For non-harmonic spectra, we count the occurrences and compute seven statistical values: sum, minimum, maximum, mean, standard deviation, variance, and the sum of squared eigenvalues. This results in eight statistical values for each of the nine atomic pairs, producing a total of 72 features for a protein in zero dimensions.
When concatenated for different dimensions of the wild type and mutant, the total 0-dimensional PL-based feature size is 1872.
When combined for different dimensions of wild type, mutant, and their differences, the total higher-dimensional PL-based feature size is 420.
With 3D PPI complexes generated by AF3, AF3-assisted MT-TopLap achieves robust predictive performance, showing minimal deviation from results obtained using high-quality experimental 3D structures.
Our model's success extends to new variants, including the SARS-CoV-2 HK.3 variant, where it achieved a Pearson correlation coefficient (PCC) of 0.81.

Our recent study on multi-task topological Laplacian (MT-TopLap) $[32]$ , a multi-task deep learning model built by integrating persistent Laplacian, auxiliary, and pre-trained ESM-2 transformer features, revealed a 5% and 21% improvement in root mean square error (RMSE) compared to earlier topology-based models like TopLapNet $[3]$ and non-topology-based models like mCSM-PPI2 $[33]$ when predicting BFE changes upon mutation.

Apart from experimental DMS data, TDL or TopNetmAb also employs the SKEMPI 2.0 database $[31]$ , an experimental database for PPI BFE changes upon mutation.

PL is also used in the latest integration between TDL and pre-trained evolutionary scale modeling (ESM) transformer features $[29]$ to predict mutation-induced protein solubility changes, establishing a state-of-the-art method for predicting protein-protein binding free energy (BFE) changes $[30]$ .
This study identified critical mutation sites at spike protein residue positions 452 and 501, which were predicted to result in significantly more infectious SARS-CoV-2 variants $[4]$ .

AF3-predicted structures can substitute experimental structures for TDL-based DMS prediction.
MT-TopLap integrates persistent Laplacian, auxiliary, and pre-trained transformer features for multi-task learning.
Validation on four SARS-CoV-2 RBD-ACE2 DMS datasets using AF3 vs. experimental structures.

Best performance on 7XB0-RBD dataset with AF3 structures, achieving PCC 0.968 and RMSE 0.338 kcal/mol.

Fine-tuned model predicts DMS for HK.3 variant with PCC 0.81 and RMSE 1.10.
Analysis of BFE changes by structural regions shows high correlations for binding interface mutations.
Analysis of BFE changes by mutation types shows predicted patterns align with experimental data, but variance is lower.
Tracking SARS-CoV-2 variants using predicted BFE changes; Q493E mutation effect reversed in KP.3 variant.
TDL requires high-quality 3D structures, which are often delayed during pandemics.
AF3-assisted MT-TopLap achieves robust predictive performance on four DMS datasets with minimal deviation from experimental structures, and extends to HK.3 variant with PCC 0.81.
